title: Unauthenticated admin API endpoints return JSON auth errors
description: Send direct HTTP requests without cookies to representative admin APIs such as /api/items, /api/sources, /api/source-rules, /api/imports/legacy/status, and /api/reports/hidayathon-live. Verify protected endpoints do not return admin data and respond with controlled JSON 401/403/404 errors rather than HTML or empty responses.
steps: Request protected admin APIs without authentication
Verify responses are JSON
Verify no protected data is exposed
Verify status codes are controlled authorization errors

title: Manual URL intake rejects malformed and private URLs
description: POST invalid payloads to /api/items/manual-url, including empty bodies, invalid URL strings, localhost/private network URLs, and credentialed URLs. Verify each request fails with a controlled JSON error and never creates a monitoring item.
steps: POST invalid manual URL payloads
POST private localhost and internal-address URLs
POST URLs containing credentials
Verify controlled 400-level JSON errors

title: Share-link public API handles invalid tokens safely
description: Call the public share-link read endpoint with malformed, random, and path-traversal-like tokens. Verify responses are controlled JSON errors, do not leak token hashes or report internals, and never return a server error.
steps: GET /api/share-links with invalid token values
Try random and traversal-like token strings
Verify 404/410-style JSON errors
Verify no token hashes or private report internals are leaked

title: Cron endpoints fail closed when not configured or when secrets are wrong
description: Call the GET-only cron APIs /api/cron/poll-sources and /api/cron/run-connectors with no Authorization header and with a wrong bearer token. If CRON_SECRET is not configured, a controlled 503 cron_not_configured JSON response is acceptable. If CRON_SECRET is configured, missing or wrong bearer tokens must return a controlled 401/403 JSON authorization error. In every case, the endpoint must not report successful work.
steps: GET cron endpoints without Authorization
Repeat GET requests with an incorrect bearer token
Verify the response is either cron_not_configured or a controlled authorization failure
Verify no successful job execution is reported

title: Client-report member APIs do not expose data anonymously
description: Request /api/client-report/hidayathon, /api/client-report/hidayathon/export-pdf, and /api/captures/nonexistent/asset without authentication. Verify member-only data is not returned to anonymous callers and responses are controlled JSON or safe redirects.
steps: GET member-only client report APIs anonymously
GET capture asset APIs anonymously
Verify protected report payloads are not returned
Verify errors are controlled and non-empty

title: Legacy import APIs default to no-write behavior when unauthenticated
description: Attempt legacy import and Supabase upsert APIs without authentication and without admin import headers. Verify real-write operations are blocked, dry-run output is not exposed anonymously, and no secret or token fields appear in responses.
steps: POST /api/imports/legacy without authentication
GET /api/imports/legacy/supabase-plan without authentication
POST /api/imports/legacy/upsert-supabase without authentication
Verify writes are blocked and secrets are absent

title: Source rule APIs validate unsupported payloads
description: POST malformed source-rule payloads and unsupported source types to /api/source-rules without relying on UI validation. Verify validation failures are JSON, stable, and do not result in partial source persistence.
steps: POST malformed source-rule JSON
POST unsupported source type values
Verify controlled validation errors
Verify no successful persistence response is returned

title: Unknown API routes return controlled JSON
description: Request several nonexistent /api paths with GET and POST. Verify responses are controlled JSON errors rather than framework HTML error pages, empty responses, or server crashes.
steps: Request nonexistent API routes
Try multiple HTTP methods
Verify status is 404 or another controlled error
Verify response body is JSON and non-empty

title: Evidence-card SVG API handles invalid IDs safely
description: Request /api/items/:id/evidence-card.svg with missing, random, and malformed item IDs. Verify the endpoint does not leak stack traces, private filesystem paths, or unrelated item data.
steps: GET evidence-card SVG with invalid item IDs
Try IDs containing URL encoded special characters
Verify controlled errors or safe SVG responses
Verify no stack trace or filesystem path leakage

title: Request methods are constrained on API endpoints
description: Use incorrect HTTP methods against selected endpoints, such as GET on mutation-only APIs and POST on read-only APIs. Verify the server returns controlled method or authorization errors and does not perform unintended mutations.
steps: Send wrong methods to mutation APIs
Send wrong methods to read APIs
Verify errors are controlled
Verify responses do not imply state mutation
